prose: Fukui reactivity indices for furan were computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used charge ±1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken directly from the provided furan.xyz file (not re-optimized at this level). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index), found at atom C5 (1-based): f⁺ = 1.9242931. The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻) is also C5, f⁻ = 0.16164. The dual descriptor is most positive at C4 and C5 (the α-carbons), consistent with furan's known α-selectivity in electrophilic aromatic substitution. integrity.trustworthy = true; all conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = sum f⁻ = 1.0000). Warnings (all informational, all passed): [1] fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge); [2] fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge); [3] schema_header: common header present. No warnings were raised beyond these informational conservation checks, all of which passed cleanly.
